Add form-based disqualifiers to prevent processed products matching fresh protein ingredients

- Add FORM_DISQUALIFIERS map: 9 protein keys (chicken, beef, pork, lamb, salmon, fish, prawn, tuna, mince) each with a list of product descriptors (crumbed, nugget, smoked, canned, marinated, etc.) that indicate a pre-prepared or processed form
- Add _hasFormDisqualifier() method using word-level singularised set matching: handles plurals ("nuggets"→"nugget") and naturally avoids false positives ("strip" does not match "striploin")
- Apply check in _termsMatch() after successful word matching; form upgrade allows through if the recipe ingredient itself names the same descriptor (e.g. ingredient "chicken schnitzel" still matches a schnitzel deal; ingredient "smoked salmon" still matches a smoked salmon deal)
- Add backend/scripts/testFormDisqualifiers.js: 50 ingredient×deal pair tests across all protein categories, 50/50 pass

feat: [TASK-2] add docker log streaming (#3)

* feat: [TASK-2] add Docker log streaming to strixlog

- Add LogSource interface and LogEntry model as the foundation for all runtime implementations
- Implement DockerSource using raw HTTP over /var/run/docker.sock (no Docker SDK)
- Stream logs from all running containers; discover new ones via /events endpoint
- Exclude self via HOSTNAME env var comparison against container ID prefix
- Add PrintLogs printer writing normalised [ts] [source] line output to stdout
- Wire DockerSource into main.go with signal.NotifyContext for graceful shutdown
- Update docker-compose.yml with socket mount and HOSTNAME env var
- Unit tests with race detector: mux frame parsing, client API, filter logic, source lifecycle
- Integration test (//go:build integration) using real randomlog container

* refactor: [TASK-2] replace raw HTTP Docker client with SDK

Switch to github.com/docker/docker v28.5.2 SDK:
- Delete client.go and mux.go (raw HTTP + 8-byte frame parser, ~200 lines)
- Delete client_test.go and mux_test.go (~250 lines)
- source.go uses ContainerList/ContainerLogs/Events from dockerclient
- stdcopy.StdCopy replaces hand-rolled frame demuxing
- filter.go updated to container.Summary SDK type
- go.mod updated with direct dependency

* better port management

* fix: [TASK-2] address PR review comments

- Move os.Stdout restore to defer in TestPrintLogs
- Add defer cancel() in TestPrintLogsStopsOnContextCancel for consistency
- Extract magic number 12 to shortIDLen constant in filter.go
- Replace time.Sleep + exec docker compose in integration_test with testcontainers-go
- Type src as source.LogSource interface in main.go to make interface usage explicit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Show exhausted PRs in Needs Attention panel with retry action (Forge-lvk) (#67)

* feat: show exhausted PRs in Needs Attention panel with retry/dismiss actions

PRs that exhaust their CI-fix (5/5), review-fix (5/5), or rebase (3/3)
attempt limits now appear in the Hearth TUI Needs Attention panel with
a clear reason string. The Retry action resets all counters and sets
status back to open so Bellows immediately re-detects and dispatches.
The Dismiss action marks the PR as closed.

Changes:
- Add rebase_count column to prs table (migration) and persist it
  through UpdatePRLifecycle
- Add ExhaustedPRs() DB query with configurable thresholds
- Add ResetPRFixCounts() and DismissExhaustedPR() DB methods
- Extend NeedsAttentionBeads() to merge exhausted PRs into results
- Add PRID/PRNumber to NeedsAttentionItem and IPC payloads to route
  retry/dismiss to the correct DB operation (retries vs prs table)
- Add config fields: max_ci_fix_attempts, max_review_fix_attempts,
  max_rebase_attempts (with defaults from shared constants)
- Add lifecycle.Manager.SetThresholds() and wire config in daemon
- Update all callers of UpdatePRLifecycle for new rebase_count param

Bead: Forge-lvk

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-lvk

- config: add Defaults() and v.SetDefault() for max_ci/review/rebase_attempts so they
  unmarshal as 5/5/3 instead of 0 when not explicitly configured
- state/db: normalize non-positive ExhaustedPRs thresholds to defaults to avoid
  matching all PRs when config zeroes slip through
- state/db: reset ci_passing=1 in ResetPRFixCounts so Bellows re-dispatches fix
  cycles when a PR was exhausted while CI was failing
- state/db: surface ExhaustedPRs error in NeedsAttentionBeads instead of silently
  swallowing it
- state/db: add GetPRByID for authoritative bead/anvil lookup
- daemon: look up PR by ID before retry/dismiss to derive bead/anvil from DB row
  rather than trusting client-supplied values; eliminates cross-targeting risk
- hearth/data: add threshold fields to DataSource; thread them into
  FetchNeedsAttention/FetchAll so the TUI uses the same limits as the daemon
- cmd/forge/hearth: load config and populate DataSource thresholds
- state/db_test: add tests for ExhaustedPRs, ResetPRFixCounts, DismissExhaustedPR,
  and NeedsAttentionBeads including exhausted-PR items

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI failures for Forge-lvk

* fix: address review comments for Forge-lvk

- Add rebase_count to base CREATE TABLE prs schema so new DBs don't
  run an extra ALTER TABLE on every startup (migration still handles
  existing DBs)
- Handle config.Load error in hearth command with a warning to stderr
  instead of silently ignoring parse/validation failures
- Take m.mu in SetThresholds to prevent data races if called concurrently
  with HandleEvent goroutines
- Fix PRByNumber query to ORDER BY id DESC so it returns the most-recent
  PR when multiple anvils share the same PR number

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
perf: skip redundant Secret Manager writes with token hash check

Add _last_saved_token_hash to track per-user token state and skip
_save_token_to_secret when access_token, refresh_token, and expires_at
haven't changed. Eliminates ~0.5s overhead per sap_query call when
the token was not refreshed during the query.

Also add Cloud Trace IAM permission guidance comment to deploy script
for enable_tracing=True.

commit.message:
fix(security): resolve 10 production audit findings

Critical:
- timing-safe comparison: replaced === with crypto.timingSafeEqual()
  in MetricsKeyGuard and AgentApiKeyGuard (prevents timing attacks)

Access control — added @Roles() to 5 unprotected controllers:
- cash-finance: owner, admin, manager
- trip-analytics: owner, admin, manager (+ PeriodQueryDto validation)
- vhm24-integration: owner, admin, manager
- investor dividends: owner, admin (+ organizationId tenant filter)
- metrics: owner, admin

Multi-tenant:
- promo-codes: duplicate check now scoped to organizationId (was global)
- investor findDividends: added organizationId filter

Data integrity:
- collections: replaced hard delete (.remove) with .softDelete()

Performance:
- Added @Index on organizationId: investor-profile, dividend-payment
- Added @Index on investorProfileId: dividend-payment
- Added @Index on organizationId: payment-refund

Infrastructure:
- Removed DB_USER/DB_PASSWORD from Grafana container environment
- Reverted ajv override (breaks ESLint 9.x internal ajv dependency)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cloud deployment: skip NSE fetch, speed up incremental updates

- Skip fetch_nse_stock_list() during updates — NSE blocks cloud server IPs.
  Use stocks already in DB from initial seed instead.
- Increase batch size from 50 to 100 for incremental updates (fewer batches)
- Reduce inter-batch delay from 2s to 0.5s for short-period downloads
- Add batch_size and batch_delay override params to download_ohlcv()
- update_prices() now defaults to batch_size=100, delay=0.5s
- Result: ~4x faster updates (23 batches × ~5s vs 45 batches × ~20s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Summarize 11-PR batch — position specs, quality reviews, skill updates (#816)

* feat: BackwardBitReader.init specs — totalBitsRemaining bound and startPos (#804)

Add the first specifications for BackwardBitReader.init:
- BackwardBitReader_init_totalBitsRemaining_lt: the initial bit count
  is strictly less than 8 * (endPos - startPos), since the sentinel
  bit is consumed during initialization
- BackwardBitReader_init_startPos_eq: init preserves the startPos argument

These complete the reader lifecycle specs (init → readBits → isFinished),
enabling downstream proofs to track bit budgets through the full pipeline.

Also makes highBitPos non-private (was private) so the sentinel position
bound (< 8) can be proved in the spec file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for BackwardBitReader.init specs session

Schematic: log sub-bead details in events for visibility (Forge-nb7) (#61)

* feat: log sub-bead details in events for Schematic decomposition (Forge-nb7)

- Add SubBead struct (ID + Title) to schematic.Result instead of bare
  string IDs, giving callers richer metadata about created sub-beads
- Log a summary schematic_done event with JSON payload listing all
  sub-bead IDs and titles
- Log individual schematic_sub_bead events per created sub-bead for
  easy scanning in the event log and TUI
- Add BeadDecomposed Teams webhook notification (notify package) so
  operators are alerted when decomposition changes the work queue
- Wire Notifier into pipeline.Params and daemon, passing it through
  to the pipeline on dispatch
- Update pipeline tests to use new SubBead struct

- Add bead_decomposed to notifications.events docs (config.go comment and
  docs/configuration.md table) so users can discover and enable it
- Change ActionSkip to ActionClarify on createSubBeads failure so the
  pipeline exits early instead of falling through to Smith with a
  partially-decomposed parent bead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: TCP traceroute SYN-ACK detection with 5ms polling

sendProbe() blocked on a single ICMP ReadFrom for the full timeout
(500ms). When the target returned a SYN-ACK instead of ICMP, the
function only checked TCP status after the read timed out, inflating
RTT to match the timeout duration.

Replace the single blocking read with a 5ms polling loop that checks
TCP connection status between short ICMP reads. This gives accurate
RTT for both intermediate hops (ICMP Time Exceeded) and target hops
(TCP SYN-ACK).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add EtherCAT Start SmartCell

Adds KinoEtherCAT.StartCell registered as "EtherCAT Start" in
Livebook's smart cell picker. Scans the bus via a temporary
EtherCAT.start/stop cycle, populates a slave table with discovered
station identities (VID/PID), and generates a formatted
EtherCAT.start/1 config using quoted AST + quoted_to_string/1.

- Application module registers the SmartCell on startup
- Slave name and driver fields are editable; driver is parsed via
  Code.string_to_quoted/1 with graceful fallback on invalid input
- Single configurable domain (id + cycle_time_us)
- State persisted in attrs; reloads in discovered state if slaves saved
